ingredients: 1 kg floury potatoes, peeled and chunked
Salt and black pepper, to taste
steps: Poach fish: put fish in a pan with 500 ml milk, add onion studded with cloves and bay leaves. Bring just to the boil, then simmer 8 minutes. Lift fish out and strain poaching milk; flake fish into a baking dish.
Hard-boil eggs for about 8 minutes, cool, peel and quarter; arrange over fish with parsley.

Make white sauce: melt 50 g butter, stir in flour 1 minute, then gradually whisk in cooled poaching milk. Bring to a boil and cook 5 minutes until thick; season with nutmeg, salt and pepper. Pour over fish.
Make topping: boil potatoes about 20 minutes, drain and mash with remaining butter and some milk.
Assemble and bake at 200 C/180 C fan/gas 6 for about 30 minutes until golden, topping with cheddar.
tips: Push mash to the edges to seal the filling; for best texture, flake fish into large chunks so it doesn't disappear into the sauce.
